evidence: Vrijen shared his recent experience with burnout and his recovery through a wellness resort stay.
Mihir shared his journey from California to NYC (moved to 'find a wife' and is now engaged).
Mihir suggested V keep manual processes (Stripe payment links) until reaching 25-30 customers.
Discussion on the importance of annual contracts for acquisition valuation.

Vrijen is planning to run a 10k race near San Juan (Toa Baja/Savannah Seca) between February 21st and 27th, 2026. The idea came from a retreat where a participant suggested setting a challenging goal in an exotic location three months out to maintain motivation.
Tiffany pitched a structured plan to drive awareness and user acquisition through 'Zo Clubs': Handpicked passionate users become 'Club Leaders.'
Leaders name their clubs, create custom crests (using Zo symbols like the Pegasus), and receive custom links with discounts/AI credits.
Zo provides merch, X badges, social shout-outs, and a monthly budget (e.g., $100-$150) for 'Zo Giving' (e.g., DoorDash dinners for club work sessions).
Leaders manage their own channels (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, etc.). Check-ins on Mondays, surveys on Fridays.
Vrijen suggested a recruitment bonus (e.g., $10 more per month per new member) to encourage propagation.
Club leadership should be a 'tap on the shoulder' privilege to prevent gaming and maintain social status, similar to crypto communities.
Vrijen proposed a 'software exchange community' or 'open-source patchwork' where clubs donate projects to a collective hall of fame.
